era_context: Late Predynastic Egypt (Naqada III period). Upper and Lower Egypt were distinct political entities. Mesopotamia was in the Early Dynastic I period. The Indus Valley Civilization was in its early urban development phase.
geographic_coverage_gaps: All regions outside Egypt and Mesopotamia
disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are documented for this year.
historiographic_note: 3154 BCE is in the Predynastic period, entirely predating written records. No historical events can be documented for this specific year. The archaeological record for the Naqada III period shows gradual cultural development, but this cannot be resolved to individual years. Modern chronology for this period is based on radiocarbon dating and stratigraphic analysis, which have margins of error of ±50–100 years or more.
